title: Native helper implementation for macOS device owner authentication
summary: Build a small Swift-based macOS helper that performs device owner authentication and authenticated Keychain reads for plaintext secret reveal flows.
user_intent: Use macOS device owner authentication for secret reveal so Touch ID and OS-managed approval methods can gate plaintext output.
inputs: Current macOS Keychain backend in src/envrcctl/keychain.py
Current secret reveal/get behavior in src/envrcctl/cli.py
outputs: Swift helper source file(s) for authenticated Keychain reads
Documented helper CLI contract (arguments, stdout, exit codes, stderr behavior)
Documented build/run instructions for local development
Defined error mapping for authentication and Keychain failures
acceptance_criteria: A native helper design is specified for macOS only.
The helper uses LocalAuthentication with deviceOwnerAuthentication.
The helper reads a Keychain generic password item by service and account only after successful OS authentication.
The helper prints the secret value to stdout only on success.
The helper never prints secret material in error output.
The helper returns non-zero exit status for authentication failure, cancellation, missing item, and invalid input.
The helper contract is specific enough for Python integration in a later task.
implementation_notes: Prefer Swift with Foundation, LocalAuthentication, and Security frameworks.
Pass the authentication context into the Keychain query via kSecUseAuthenticationContext.
Support service/account input only; avoid extra flags unless clearly needed.
Do not rely on TTY checks or sudo.
Treat Apple Watch as OS-selected behavior under deviceOwnerAuthentication rather than an explicit factor choice.
risks: Packaging the helper with the Python distribution may add complexity.
Apple Watch approval behavior may vary across macOS versions and host configuration.
Existing Keychain item creation flow may not yet use item-level access control.

automated: Static review of helper CLI contract
Mocked integration expectations for Python caller
manual: Successful auth via Touch ID on a supported Mac
Cancellation path returns failure without revealing the secret
Missing Keychain item returns failure with sanitized error
Apple Watch-assisted approval succeeds when offered by macOS

non_goals: Migrating stored secrets to SecAccessControl-protected items in this task
Changing inject or exec behavior in this task
notes: This task defines and implements the native helper itself, not the Python CLI integration.
If legacy plaintext flags remain temporarily, they should be wired in a later integration task to this authenticated path.
